remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Governance as acceleration: data proves it’s not a speed bump
remarks.content: For years, the enterprise AI governance conversation has been shaped by a quiet assumption: Governance is the thing that slows AI down. This may look like the review cycle that delays the launch, committee that questions the model, or even documentation requirement that sits between a wo
remarks.contentTranslation: 多年来，企业AI治理对话一直被一个安静的假设所塑造：治理是减缓AI的因素。这可能看起来像延迟发布的审核周期、质疑模型的委员会，甚至是WO之间的文档要求
remarks.translation: 治理作为加速：数据证明它不是减速带

remarks.text: Generative AI governance framework: building responsible AI systems
remarks.content: Generative AI is no longer a pilot that leaders can observe from a distance. It is in contact centers, developer workflows, reporting layers, and autonomous agents acting on behalf of the business. As BARC's research on modernizing governance for the era of agentic AI shows, the stakes r
remarks.contentTranslation: 生成式AI不再是领导者可以远距离观察的试点。它存在于联系中心、开发人员工作流、报告层和代表业务行事的自主Agent中。正如BARC关于智能AI时代治理现代化的研究所表明的那样，

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Sovereignty isn't where your AI runs. It's whether it answers to you.
remarks.content: Last week, a government order took the world's best AI model offline in hours. No one deleted it and no server failed. A single directive put the most capable model on the market out of reach for everyone not a citizen of one country.
remarks.contentTranslation: 上周，一项政府命令在数小时内让世界上最好的AI模型下线。没有人将其删除，也没有服务器发生故障。单一指令使市场上最有能力的模型对每个人不是一个国家的公民来说都是遥不可及的。
remarks.translation: 主权并不是你的AI运行的地方。问题在于它是否对你有所回应。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: What is an AI orchestration layer? Architecture, benefits, and enterprise use cases
remarks.content: AI investments are multiplying, but coordination between models, agents, and data pipelines remains an afterthought in most enterprises. The result is predictable: duplicated workflows, inconsistent outputs, ungoverned decisions, and AI initiatives that never graduate from pilot to produ
remarks.contentTranslation: AI投资正在成倍增加，但在大多数企业中，模型、Agent和数据管道之间的协调仍然是事后的想法。结果是可预测的：重复的工作流、不一致的输出、不受控制的决策以及从试点到生产的AI计划
remarks.translation: 什么是AI编排层？架构、优势和企业用例

remarks.text: AI explainability in enterprise AI: methods, tools, and why it matters for trustworthy models and agents
remarks.content: AI models, generative AI (GenAI) applications, and agents are making decisions that affect credit scores, patient care, and hiring outcomes. Enterprises deploying these systems are accumulating regulatory and reputational risk proportional to the number of decisions that cannot be explai
remarks.contentTranslation: AI模型、生成式AI(GenAI)应用程序和Agent商正在做出影响信用评分、患者护理和招聘结果的决策。部署这些系统的企业正在积累与无法解释的决策数量成比例的监管和声誉风险
remarks.translation: 企业AI中的AI可解释性：方法、工具及其对可信模型和Agent的重要性

remarks.content: Most vendors claim their agentic AI platform is enterprise-grade. In a market where the term has lost almost all meaning, the real test is not the demo. It is what happens after, when agents meet real data, real governance requirements, and the organizational complexity that no sandbox c

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Launched a side project quietly a few weeks ago.. 

Wanted to test how many impressions/clicks I could get by simply having Claude Code / Codex optimize the hell out of the website.

Got it to about 16k impressions, and 94 clicks over the last 28 days. Not bad eh?

No backlinks, no mention of this website anywhere on my socials, no Reddit hacking - simply old school SEO/GEO optimization. 

Official launch soon - parents will like this one 🍩
remarks.translation: 几周前悄悄启动了一个辅助项目。想测试一下，只要让Claude Code/Codex优化网站，我可以获得多少展示/点击。在过去的28天里，它获得了大约16000次展示和94次点击。不错吧？没有反向链接，在我的社交媒体上任何地方都没有提到这个网站，没有Reddit黑客-只是老派的SEO/地理优化。即将正式推出-家长会喜欢这个 🍩
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The fact that open weights models are being discussed credibly at this level of capability should be a huge update for many. 

The implications of open models getting to frontier performance ensures that you can always have sovereign AI, have the ability to post train for your specific workflows, cost optimize for various workloads, and actually afford to do much more with AI (which opens up meaningfully different applications).

Huge win for the applied AI layer.
remarks.translation: 在这种能力水平上，开放权重模型正在被可靠地讨论，这对许多人来说应该是一个巨大的更新。开放模型达到前沿性能的含义确保您始终可以拥有主权AI，能够为您的特定工作流发布培训，针对各种工作负载进行成本优化，并且实际上能够使用AI做更多的事情（这开辟了有意义的不同应用程序）。应用AI层的巨大胜利。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This is a good update for getting access to Fable. It also gives us a view into what the future is likely going to look like with AI regulation. 

The government will have frameworks that are used to determine future model releases past a certain threshold of capability or compute levels. Given all the constituents involved, and the economic and societal significance of AI, this was practically an inevitability.

It may seem small but the implications are massive. It will mean that each model update will go through an extensive review, testing, and feedback process. And in that processes lots of groups will weigh in on the risk of the model, and there will be lots of subjectivity on what the actual risks are or practicalities of exploiting those risks. 

A positive potential future here would be we still get massive model progress but they just happen in bigger jumps at once, where the labs pack in major improvements since the cost and slow down of each review stacks up. 

On the other side, the risk is that past a certain threshold we may not get to see the rapid back and forth of model progress that we’ve gotten used to which can have negative compounding effects. Hoping for the former outcome.
remarks.translation:
这是获得寓言访问权限的一个很好的更新。它还让我们看到了AI监管的未来可能是什么样子。政府将拥有用于确定未来模型发布超过一定能力或计算水平阈值的框架。考虑到所涉及的所有成分，以及AI的经济和社会意义，这实际上是不可避免的。它可能看起来很小，但影响是巨大的。
这意味着每个模型更新都将经过广泛的审查、测试和反馈过程。在这个过程中，许多团体将权衡模型的风险，并且在利用这些风险的实际风险或实用性方面存在很多主观性。
在这里，一个积极的潜在未来将是我们仍然可以获得巨大的模型进展，但它们只是一次性发生更大的跳跃，因为每次审查的成本和速度都会增加，因此实验室会进行重大改进。另一方面，风险在于，超过一定的阈值，我们可能无法看到我们已经习惯的模型进度的快速来回，这可能会产生负复合效应。希望能有前一种结果。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Nan Yu
remarks.role: head of product linear
remarks.text:
It took us a lot of iterations to find the right pattern for this. Early on we were very naive and we thought that the agent could one shot project updates without much input from users.

That approach ended up creating a bunch of slop, because users disengaged their brain from the process of writing an update.

By switching to a multi turn interactive mode, the agent prompts you and asks you what to emphasize, what's most important, what context is missing, etc.

This simple change elicits much better project updates and ensures that the user has a chance to steer rather than simply being asked to say yes to everything.

A very good example of introducing friction for a net benefit.
remarks.translation:
我们花了很多次迭代才找到合适的模式。在早期，我们非常天真，我们认为Agent可以一次性更新项目，而无需用户太多输入。这种方法最终造成了一堆麻烦，因为用户在编写更新的过程中脱离了他们的大脑。通过切换到多回合互动模式，客服代表会提示您要强调什么、最重要的是什么、缺少什么背景等。
这个简单的变化可以引发更好的项目更新，并确保用户有机会引导，而不是简单地被要求对所有事情都说“是”。引入摩擦以获得净收益的一个很好的例子。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Nan Yu
remarks.role: head of product linear
remarks.text:
it is making the right choice from a range of plausible options along many dimensions with limited information... when a chess or go player intuits a move without calculating, they are exercising taste

https://t.co/044iRz1wQf
remarks.translation: 它在信息有限的许多维度上从一系列合理的选择中做出正确的选择……当国际象棋或围棋选手在没有计算的情况下直觉地移动时，他们正在锻炼品味

remarks.name: Matt Turck
remarks.role: VC at FirstMarkCap.  Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
remarks.text:
State of AI compute 2026: my conversation with @stephenbalaban of @LambdaAPI on the neocloud boom, data centers, GPUs and what's ahead

00:00 — Cold open
01:21 — Why GPU compute was never a commodity
02:45 — The H100 price index and what it gets wrong
04:02 — The real moat: technology or financing?
05:57 — Winner-take-all, or room for many neoclouds
06:48 — Are we overbuilding or underbuilding AI compute?
09:26 — What if AI gets 10x more compute-efficient?
10:44 — The real bottleneck: land, power, and shell
11:38 — The backlash against data centers — and the misinformation
15:00 — Opening the hood: from photons to tokens
17:11 — Extracting more value from the same chip
19:26 — Frontier inference and distributed training, explained
23:26 — What actually drives compute cost
25:21 — Lambda's chip stack and the NVIDIA relationship
26:17 — A multi-silicon world? CUDA, CUDNN, and NVIDIA's real moat
28:59 — Networking, storage, and the one-click cluster
34:46 — Renting vs. owning, and full vertical integration
36:24 — How global is Lambda? Does location still matter?
38:44 — The financing stack: off-take agreements, SPVs, and credit
41:16 — Why a 2023 GPU leases for more today
42:36 — A futures market for compute?
43:54 — Origin story: facial recognition, Perceptio, and Apple
47:03 — The Lambda hat and Dream Scope
48:59 — The $60K bet that became a cloud business
52:00 — Holding the team together through the hard times
54:30 — Bringing on a new CEO; Stephen as CTO
57:33 — Matching xAI on high-velocity deployment
59:29 — "AI won't write software — it will become the software"
01:01:30 — Neural software vs. vibe coding
01:04:25 — Do agents change the compute layer
01:06:14 — Self-assembling software inside Lambda
01:08:18 — Gigawatt-scale AI factories
01:08:57 — One person, one GPU
01:12:04 — Hot takes: overrated and underrated in AI
remarks.translation:
2026年AI计算状况：我与@ LambdaAPI的@ stephenbalaban关于新云热潮、数据中心、GPU和未来发展的对话00:00 —冷开01:21 —为什么GPU计算从未成为商品02:45 — H100价格指数及其出错之处04:02 —真正的护城河：技术还是融资？ 05:57 —赢家通吃，还是许多新云的空间06:48 —我们是否过度构建或不足构建AI计算？ 09:26 —如果AI使计算效率提高10倍呢？
10:44 —真正的瓶颈：土地、电力和外壳11:38 —对数据中心的强烈反对—以及错误信息15:00 —打开引擎盖：从光子到代币17:11 —从同一芯片中提取更多价值19:26 —前沿推理和分布式培训， 23:26 —实际驱动计算成本25:21 — Lambda的芯片堆栈和NVIDIA的关系26:17 —多芯片世界？

43:54 —起源故事：面部识别、Perceptio和Apple 47:03 — The Lambda HAT和Dream Scope 48:59 — 6万美元的赌注变成了云业务52:00 —在艰难时期将团队团结在一起54:30 —带来新的首席执行官； Stephen作为CTO 57:33 —在高速部署中匹配xAI 59:29 — “AI不会编写软件—它将成为软件” 01:01:30 —神经软件与

https://t.co/L8j4FFUkOW

remarks.text:
Bernie Sanders introduced a bill to seize 50% of any AI startup that crosses $200M in revenue. 

The same anti-prosperity bloc spent the year trying to ban startup acquisitions, blocking the only exit 85% of founders ever get. 

This is a war on building startups in America. https://t.co/zwYL1wlLHO
remarks.translation: 伯尼·桑德斯（ Bernie Sanders ）提出了一项法案，以扣押超过2亿美元收入的AI初创企业的50%。同一个反繁荣集团花了一年时间试图禁止初创企业收购，阻止了85%的创始人获得的唯一退出。这是一场在美国建立初创公司的战争。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
For people who don’t have a clear sense of the future they want, AI is just another mechanism of control

But in the hands of someone with agency, AI is the breaker of chains, something that lets you do things no humans can do alone. 

AI can be a liberator if you choose agency.
remarks.translation: 对于那些对未来没有清晰认识的人来说，AI只是另一种控制机制，但在拥有Agent权的人手中，AI是链条的断路器，可以让你做一些人类无法独自完成的事情。如果你选择机构，AI可以成为解放者。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 88: Unpacking DeepMind's Quest for SuperIntelligence with Demis Hassabis' Biographer
podcasts.excerpt: Sebastian Mallaby spent three years and 30+ hours interviewing Demis Hassabis in the back of a British pub to write The Infinity Machine, and the conversation uses that reporting to surface the most underexplored figure in AI. Demis founded the original AI lab in 2010, won a Nobel Prize, runs models that consistently top the leaderboards, and yet remains so unrecognized that Sebastian's own publisher worried no one would buy a book with his face on the cover. The throughline is a paradox: Demis tried to prevent the AI race we're now all living through, and now finds himself one of its central protagonists. He used to believe a single lab could carry the safety burden to AGI; he now sees safety as a collective action problem only governments can solve. He hedged DeepMind's research bets across every promising direction, and as a result missed the two most consumer-defining moments…
